## PAGE 01232: Symbian macro

- page_id: `136715643`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715643/Symbian+macro
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:17:40.925+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Code Engineering > C++ Code Engineering
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following macro definitions have been added to the *explicit* macro list:

```cpp
name = {sizeof(s) -1,s}

#define _LIT8(name,s) const static TListC name = {sizeof(s) -1,s}

#define IMPORT_C /*__declspec(dllimport)*/

#define EXPORT_C /*__declspec(dllexport)*/

#ut NONSHARABLE_CLASS(x) class x

#define NONSHARABLE_STRUCT(x) struct x

#define GLREF_D extern

#define GLDEF_D

#define LOCAL_D static

#define GLREF_C extern

#define GLDEF_C

#define LOCAL_C static]]>
```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span>The following macro definitions have been added to the <em>explicit</em> macro list:</span></p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="09b7e4d7-d75d-47ec-8d60-ba60969bf704"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">cpp</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[// Symbian macro
#define _L(string) TPtrC((const TText*) string)

#define _LIT(name,s) const static TListC<sizeof(s)> name = {sizeof(s) -1,s}

#define _LIT8(name,s) const static TListC<sizeof(s)> name = {sizeof(s) -1,s}

#define IMPORT_C /*__declspec(dllimport)*/

#define EXPORT_C /*__declspec(dllexport)*/

#ut NONSHARABLE_CLASS(x) class x

#define NONSHARABLE_STRUCT(x) struct x

#define GLREF_D extern

#define GLDEF_D

#define LOCAL_D static

#define GLREF_C extern

#define GLDEF_C

#define LOCAL_C static]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711642 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01233: Symbol Properties dialog

- page_id: `136711642`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711642/Symbol+Properties+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:48.406+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Formatting symbols
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Warning

390170097

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Warning

390170110

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Warning

390170100

In the **Symbol Properties** dialog, you may define various symbol (or group of symbols) properties, such as fill color, font type, whether to wrap text. The **Symbol Properties** dialog is a non-modal window - you can edit the symbol properties and see the changes on the diagram instantly. In addition, while the dialog is active you can still work with diagrams or other dialogs.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To open the **Symbol Properties** dialog

1. On the diagram, select a symbol or a group of symbols.
2. Do one of the following:
  - From the main menu, select Edit > Symbol > Symbol Properties .
  - From the selected elements' shortcut menu, choose Symbol Properties .
  - Press Alt+Enter.

In the following table, you will find the described common functions that **Symbol Properties** dialog provides.

| Icon | Title | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | Apply Style | Button | Click to open the menu with the available . |
|  | Show All Properties | Button | Click to display all the properties for the selected symbol types.Note that the Show All Properties button is displayed only when at least two shapes of different element types are selected, for example, a class and a package. |
|  | Make Default | Button | Click to change the default symbol properties to the properties that are specified in this dialog.WarningIt is highly recommended to use the Make Default button only if you fully understand the symbol styles and how your symbol styles can be affected.WarningThe Make Default button is available in the Teamwork Cloud project only if you have locked the symbol styles for edit. |

If your project is of the earlier version you need to set up a new symbol properties manually.

390170095

**Related pages**

****

- Symbol Properties dialog
- Applying image
- Displaying icon or image
  - Displaying rake icon
- Symbol styles option group
- Working with Symbol styles
- Properties Inheritance
- Stereotype properties
  - Collecting symbol styles from modules
- Copy and paste symbol style
- Making your own default style for new projects

Properties</strong></td><td>Button</td><td><p>Click to display all the properties for the selected symbol types.</p><p>Note that the <em>Show All Properties</em> button is displayed only when at least two shapes of different <br />element types are selected, for example, a class and a package.</p></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment Default</strong></td><td>Button</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Click to change the default symbol properties to the properties that are specified in this dialog.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f7eb2419-8adf-4903-a981-b9dfe71cefe2"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Warning</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>It is highly recommended to edit.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr></tbody></table><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="848a1d08-339e-4610-b7af-b7b53df89ebf"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If your project is of the earlier version you need to set up a new symbol properties <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136714928 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01234: Symbol Standard Expert Configuration

- page_id: `136714928`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136714928/Symbol+Standard+Expert+Configuration
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:16:57.529+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > UML Profiling and DSL Guide > Domain Specific Language customization > Creating Customization Data > Customization Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Using the **Symbol Standard Expert Configuration** property, you can specify properties visibility mode:

- in the DSL element [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='MD2024x'] ,
- in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing Element Shortcut Menu' space='MD2024x'] , and
- in menus that open after clicking the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Compartments' space='MD2024x'] or Create Element smart manipulators.

To customize Standard Expert mode

1. Create a customization element for the DSL element or select an existing one.
2. In the element [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'] , click the Symbol Standard Expert Configuration property and then click the button.
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dialog for customizing selected modeling tool area' space='MD2024x']**Customize Properties** dialog , click to select the mode to display the property in the DSL element Symbol Properties dialog and in other places.
4. Reopen the project.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Customization Data' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Customization Data' space='MD2024x']

and</li><li>in menus that open after clicking the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Compartments" /></ac:link> or Create Element smart manipulators.</li></ul><p><br <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711666 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01235: Symbol styles option group

- page_id: `136711666`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711666/Symbol+styles+option+group
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:49.215+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Formatting symbols
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

All symbols in MagicDraw are created according to active properties styles. There may be more than one property style in the same project, and the whole style may be applied for the project.

Every style has its own presentation of Diagram, Shape, Path, and Stereotype that you can modify using the **Project Options** dialog in the **Symbol styles** option group. You can set your own options for every model element to the current style.

**Path**, **Shape**, and **Stereotype** branches have the inner structure that helps you find the model element, the representation of which must be changed. The section on the right side of the dialog contains possible choices and instruments to manage them.

The following properties are defined for the formatting symbols:

- Shapes . Set general options for the shapes in the right pane of the Project Options dialog. You can set options for all shapes that appear on the Diagram pane.
- Paths . Set general options for the paths in the right pane of the Project Options dialog. You can set options for all paths that appear on the Diagram pane.
- Diagram . Set general options about a diagram.
- Stereotypes . Set general options for the stereotypes in the right pane of the Project Options dialog. You can set options for all stereotypes that may be applied to elements on the Diagram pane.

##### Changing options for multiple symbols

To change properties for multiple symbols, press Ctrl or Shift and select few symbols in the **Project Options** dialog > **Symbol styles** option group.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Shape, path, and diagram options

All shapes, paths and diagrams that can be created in the project, are listed in the **Project Options** dialog. If the Shape, Path, or Diagram branches are selected in the tree, the general properties can be set in the right pane of this dialog.

When expanding any of these option groups, the style for a particular element (diagram) can be created.

To apply a new style to a previously created element symbol

1. In the Project Options dialog , change the element style properties and click the Apply button. The Select Diagrams dialog opens.
2. Select the diagrams to which the element properties will be applied and click OK . The Select Properties to Apply dialog opens.
3. Select the properties to be applied to the element symbol by moving them from the All list to the Selected list.
4. Click **OK** when you are done. If a new style was set, it will be applied for all newly created elements after drawing them on the diagram pane. You can always set the default symbols style to the element by clicking the **Apply Default Symbol Style** button in the main toolbar.

##### Properties extension by diagram

Diagram, shape, path, and stereotype properties can be extended by the particular diagram type. This means that presentation style options will be applied only for the specified element symbol in the specific diagram.

To specify element properties for the selected diagram type

1. In the Project Options dialog, the Symbol Styles option group, do one of the following
  - Selected the desired element and from its shortcut menu, select the diagram type. The diagram is added as an additional branch to the section. [ATTACHMENT filename='extend_by_diagram_1.png']
  - Click the Extend by Diagram button, in the open Extend by Diagram dialog, click the Add Diagram button and select the desired diagram type.
2. Set the style properties for the element. The properties will be applied only in the specified type of diagram.

To remove the extended diagram from the tree, do one of the following

- In the Project Options dialog, select the extended diagram and from the shortcut menu, choose Remove .
- In the Project Options dialog, select the extended diagram and click the Remove button on the right.

#### PANEL: On this page

On this page

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710417 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01236: Sync with Excel or CSV files

- page_id: `136710417`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710417/Sync+with+Excel+or+CSV+files
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:12:38.148+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Tables
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Write To File exceptions

76498961

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Write To File exceptions

76498964

76643702

**On this page**

43

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Write To File exceptions

76498963

top

##### Introduction

You can transfer data between Excel/CSV files and modeling tool tables. After sync, the data on the Excel file of the selected sheet or CSV file matches the data in the modeling tool table. This mechanism is bidirectional and ensures continuous work between Excel/CSV files and modeling tool tables. If you don't have the ability to collaborate via the internet but you need to share information through Excel or CSV files, you can use this feature. The Excel/CSV file syncing is available in all tables of the modeling tool by using the **Excel/CSV** **Sync**button [IMAGE alt='' src=''] in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Table toolbars' space='MD2024x']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Preparing Excel or CSV files to sync

To sync data from Excel or CSV files, the files must meet the following prerequisites:

- The heading is located in the first row.
- When creating relationships, the spreadsheet must contain the source and target columns. The source and target columns contain the names/qualified names of elements respectively that are the client and supplier of the relationship.

Recommended prerequisites:

- Eliminate merged cells.
- Every column has a unique column name.
- Every row has the same number and name of columns. Every cell has the element name or qualified element name.

##### How to synchronize your data

To synchronize the table with the Excel or CSV file

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating an empty table' space='MD2024x'] or open an existing table.
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Table Criteria area' space='MD2024x'] , select the Element Type or Classifier .
3. Click the **Columns** button and select properties and/or slots to which you want to sync data from the Excel or CSV file.
4. On the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Table toolbars' space='MD2024x'], click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and then choose **Excel/CSV File** > **Select File**. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Excel and CSV Sync Options dialog' space='MD2024x']**Excel/CSV Sync Options** dialog, select the Excel or CSV file either from your file system or from the model, if the file is already attached. [How to attach the file to a project >](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Attaching%2C+reattaching%2C+and+detaching+files) 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] An alternative way to link Excel or CSV file to the table is to drag it onto the table, hold it down until the Tooltip appears (long drag), then drop it, and select either**Link Excel/CSV File**or**Attach and Link Excel/CSV File**. If you want to modify sync options, select on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Table toolbars' space='MD2024x'], click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] ,then choose **Excel/CSV File** > **Sync Options**and go to step 6. If you want to synchronize data instantly in the ordinal position, go to step 9. - If you select another file, e.g. Vehicle Requirements.xlsx on a modeling tool table that is already linked with the file, e.g. Stakeholder Requirements.xlsx, the previous file named *Stakeholder Requirements.xlsx* is replaced with the latest one *Vehicle Requirements.xlsx*. 
- You can link only one Excel/CSV file with the same modeling tool table.
5. Click Details to expand the dialog.
6. The modeling tool might require refreshing the dialog before setting the mappings. Therefore, if you see this notification at the bottom of the dialog, click **Refresh**. Before uploading the mapping of the older version to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Teamwork Cloud Documentation' space='TWCloud2024x'], you need to recreate the mapping in the local project and resave it first.
7. In the opened **Excel/CSV Sync Options** dialog select options. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Excel and CSV Sync Options dialog' space='MD2024x']>]]> 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] If you are importing requirements, they are first mapped by ID if the ID column is displayed. If the ID column is not displayed, then the requirements are mapped by name. If the name is changed, then a new requirement is created in the table.
8. Click OK .
9. Import data from the linked file in one of the following ways: Additional optional procedures prior to data import[Using UML properties upon import](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Managing+Excel+and+CSV+file#ManagingExcelandCSVfile-UsingUMLpropertiesuponimportUsingUMLpropertiesuponimport)[Skipping empty rows and/or columns upon import](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Managing+Excel+and+CSV+file#ManagingExcelandCSVfile-Skippingemptyrowsand/orcolumnsuponimport) - The notification appears on the down-right corner of the modeling tool window (see the following figure), click **Read from File**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
-On the table toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select the **Read From File** command. Read From File exceptionsAfter making any changes in the Excel or CSV file you must save them before using the**Read From File**command. Otherwise, the changes will not be imported to the modeling tool table.Data cannot be imported or imported correctly if:The CSV file format is invalid.Cells are non-editable.Data types of columns are not compatible with source information.Columns do not support information updates.Elements with the same names but different types exist.The owner is not set or updated because this element does not exist in the model.Also, some text formatting may not be transferred.
10. If you synchronize requirements, in the **Select Type** dialog, select the element you want to create in the modeling tool table.
11. Wait while data are imported into the table. The relevant notification displays in the down-right corner, when synchronization is completed. Items synchronized to the SysML requirements table appear in the Model Browser, too. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] If you made changes in the file and saved them, the table[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Legends' space='MD2024x'] displays automatically and highlights the **New**, **Obsolete**, **Unchanged**, and **Updated** rows. In the figure above, the first requirement is updated, the seventh is newly created, and the second is deleted. All the items in the table are highlighted. The legend above reveals that all new items are highlighted like this. You can change the legend colors or hide the legend from the table. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Legends' space='MD2024x']>]]>
12. After making changes in the modeling tool table, export that data to the linked file as follows: on the table toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select the **Write To File** command. Additional optional procedures prior to data export[Mapping elements by ID upon export](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Managing+Excel+and+CSV+file#ManagingExcelandCSVfile-ToexportbyIDMappingelementsbyIDuponexport)[Color marking modified data upon export](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Managing+Excel+and+CSV+file#ManagingExcelandCSVfile-Colormarkingmodifieddatauponexport) Write To File exceptionsThe data cannot be exported or exported correctly if:The Excel file is corrupted.The CSV file is open.The CSV file is locked or you do not have the necessary permissions to access it. The data is synchronized between the modeling tool table and Excel or CSV file.

]]>[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sync with Excel or CSV files' space='MD2024x']

76498960

**Related pages**

****

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Tables' space='']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Legends' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing data from Excel or CSV files' space='MD2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4256d846-996a-488a-95ab-29f455db55af"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">76498961</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aeee9d67-29a6-440c-b370-c73513f65632"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">76498964</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="afc2dc81-9c3a-498e-9ff5-a43b5c629c56"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">76643702</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ea499124-a3a5-4a77-89e7-b44c25bba24d"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="198bc142-dbeb-41f8-be5c-f09115ba64a2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">76498963</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a814c1ff-dd22-4e23-b60b-a8bda3edd769"><ac:parameter ac:name="">top</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h3>Introduction</h3><p>You can transfer data between Excel/CSV files and modeling tool tables. <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7b606a67-c95d-452c-b6b9-71510ccc6595">After sync, the data on the Excel file of the selected sheet or CSV file matches the data in the modeling tool table.</ac:inline-comment-marker> This mechanism is bidirectional and ensures continuous work between Excel/CSV files and modeling tool tables. If you don't have the ability to collaborate via the internet but you need to share information through Excel or CSV files, you can use this feature. The Excel/CSV file syncing is available in all tables of the modeling tool by using the <strong>Excel/CSV</strong> <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><strong>Sync </strong></span>button <ac:image ac:title="Excel/CSV Sync" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="excel_csv_sync.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Table toolbars" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[table toolbar.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:queryparams="effects=border-simple,shadow-kn" ac:height="253" ac:width="253"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Excel Linking dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Sync with Excel or CSV files" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3 class="with-breadcrumbs">Preparing Excel or CSV files to sync</h3><p>To sync data from Excel or CSV files, the files must meet the following p<span style="color: rgb(57,57,60);">rerequisites</span>:</p><ul><li>The heading is located in the first row.</li><li>When creating relationships, the spreadsheet must contain the source and target columns. The source and target columns contain the names/qualified names of elements respectively that are the client and supplier of the relationship.</li></ul><p>Recommended prerequisites:</p><ul><li>Eliminate merged cells.</li><li>Every column has a unique column name.</li><li>Every row has the same number and name of columns.<br />Every cell has the element name or qualified element name. </li></ul><h3>How to synchronize your data</h3><p>To synchronize the table with the Excel or CSV file</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating an empty table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create new]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or open an existing table.</li><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Table Criteria area" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[table Criteria area]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, select the <strong>Element Type</strong> or <strong>Classifier</strong>.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click the <strong>Columns</strong> button and select properties and/or slots to which you want to sync data from the Excel or CSV file.</span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">On the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Table toolbars" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[table toolbar]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, click <ac:image ac:title="Excel/CSV Sync" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="excel_csv_sync.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Sync with Excel or CSV files" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and then choose <strong>Excel/CSV File</strong> &gt; <strong>Select File</strong>. In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Excel and CSV Sync Options dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Excel/CSV Sync Options</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>, select the Excel or CSV file either from your file system or from the model, if the file is already attached. <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> </span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Attaching%2C+reattaching%2C+and+detaching+files" rel="nofollow">How to attach the file to a project &gt;</a><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="selecting_file_to_sync.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Sync with Excel or CSV files" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="757a0bb7-741b-4fdb-a96b-16c06266b303"><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">An alternative way to link Excel or CSV file to the table is to drag it onto the table, hold it down until the Tooltip appears (long drag), then d</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">rop it, and select either </span><strong>Link Excel/CSV File </strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">or</span><strong> Attach and Link Excel/CSV File</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. If you want to modify sync options, select on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Table toolbars" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[table toolbar]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, click <ac:image ac:title="Excel/CSV Sync" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="excel_csv_sync.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Sync with Excel or CSV files" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> ,then choose <strong>Excel/CSV File</strong> &gt; <strong>Sync Options </strong>and go to step 6. If you want to synchronize data instantly in the ordinal position, go to step 9.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2ddb2eed-5c4a-4d08-9d70-79b037b2629a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>- If you select another file, e.g. Vehicle Requirements.xlsx on a modeling tool table that is already linked with the file, e.g. Stakeholder Requirements.xlsx, the previous file named <em>Stakeholder Requirements.xlsx</em> is replaced with the latest one <em>Vehicle Requirements.xlsx</em>.<br /><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">- You can link only one Excel/CSV file with the same modeling tool table.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Click <strong>Details</strong> to expand the dialog.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">The modeling tool might require refreshing the dialog before setting the mappings. Therefore, if you see this notification at the bottom of the dialog, click <strong>Refresh</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dc32914e-2e0f-4a7f-a554-54b87d7b0e4c"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span><span>Before uploading the mapping of the older version to <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloud2024x" ri:content-title="Teamwork Cloud Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Teamwork Cloud]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, you need to recreate the mapping in the local project and resave it first.</span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the opened <strong>Excel/CSV Sync Options</strong> dialog select options. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Excel and CSV Sync Options dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about sync options >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="specifying_sync_options_v2.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Sync with Excel or CSV files" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="01a061d7-847d-4e1e-8e2a-7e50afb0709f"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you are importing requirements, they are first mapped by ID if the ID column is displayed. If the ID column is not displayed, then the requirements are mapped by name. If the name is changed, then a new requirement is created in the table.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Import data from the linked file in one of the following ways:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="231f1b0a-d1c5-4a3f-a25b-2598fb14d149"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Additional optional procedures prior to data import</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Managing+Excel+and+CSV+file#ManagingExcelandCSVfile-UsingUMLpropertiesuponimportUsingUMLpropertiesuponimport">Using UML properties upon import</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Managing+Excel+and+CSV+file#ManagingExcelandCSVfile-Skippingemptyrowsand/orcolumnsuponimport">Skipping empty rows and/or columns upon import</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">- The notification appears on the down-right corner of the modeling tool window (see the following figure), click <strong>Read from File</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:height="98"><ri:attachment ri:filename="excel_sync_options_notification.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Sync with Excel or CSV files" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper">- </span>On the table toolbar, click  <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper confluence-embedded-manual-size"><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="excel_csv_sync.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Sync with Excel or CSV files" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and s</span>elect the <strong>Read From File</strong> command.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5700a63e-4f11-460c-8536-10ac29aa9221"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Read From File exceptions </ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">After making any changes in the Excel or CSV file you must save them before using the </span><strong>Read From File</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> command. Otherwise, the changes will not be imported to the modeling tool table.</span></p><p>Data cannot be imported or imported correctly if:</p><ul><li>The CSV file format is invalid.</li><li>Cells are non-editable.</li><li>Data types of columns are not compatible with source information.</li><li>Columns do not support information updates.</li><li>Elements with the same names but different types exist.</li><li>The owner is not set or updated because this element does not exist in the model.</li></ul><p>Also, some text formatting may not be transferred.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">If you synchronize requirements, in the <strong>Select Type</strong> dialog, select the element you want to create in the modeling tool table.</p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Wait while data are imported into the table. The relevant notification displays in the down-right corner, when synchronization is completed. Items synchronized to the SysML requirements table appear in the Model Browser, too.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="read_from_excel_after_changes_example.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Sync with Excel or CSV files" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3eb5372d-a14b-4270-806b-2c3b50ed84f6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you made changes in the file and saved them, the table<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Legends" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ Legend]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> displays automatically and highlights the <strong>New</strong>, <strong>Obsolete</strong>, <strong>Unchanged</strong>, and <strong>Updated</strong> rows. In the figure above, the first requirement is updated, the seventh is newly created, and the second is deleted. All the items in the table are highlighted. The legend above reveals that all new items are highlighted like this. You can change the legend colors or hide the legend from the table. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Legends" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about legends >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">After making changes in the modeling tool table, export that data to the linked file as follows: on the table toolbar, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper confluence-embedded-manual-size"><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="excel_csv_sync.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Sync with Excel or CSV files" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and s</span>elect the <strong>Write To File</strong> command.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9c8f55ff-c82a-422e-a84a-9580401b1389"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Additional optional procedures prior to data export</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Managing+Excel+and+CSV+file#ManagingExcelandCSVfile-ToexportbyIDMappingelementsbyIDuponexport">Mapping elements by ID upon export</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Managing+Excel+and+CSV+file#ManagingExcelandCSVfile-Colormarkingmodifieddatauponexport">Color marking modified data upon export</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="11c7a2a7-93bc-45c9-8d6b-482ec303e615"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Write To File exceptions </ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The data cannot be exported or exported correctly if:</p><ul><li>The Excel file is corrupted.</li><li><p>The CSV file is open.</p></li><li><p>The CSV file is locked or you do not have the necessary permissions to access it.</p></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">The data is synchronized between the modeling tool table and Excel or CSV file.</p></li></ol><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top >]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Sync with Excel or CSV files" /></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="17789302-6bdf-454b-99b9-6bf14bff427e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">76498960</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><strong><br /></strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="ac941ff6-9670-4e8f-8329-c9d42b30b272"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Tables" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Legends" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Importing data from Excel or CSV files" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711844 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01237: Synchronization dialog environment

- page_id: `136711844`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711844/Synchronization+dialog+environment
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:59.030+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Parameters synchronization
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Synchronization** dialog represents those elements whose parameters are not synchronized and provides the ability to restore the synchronization.

In this section, you will find brief information for opening the **Synchronization** dialog and for each button that can be useful while working in the dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To open the Synchronization dialog, do one of the following

- In the Model Browser, right-click the invalid element. On the shortcut menu, point to Validation , then to the validation group, and click the Synchronize Manually command.
- On the diagram pane, select the invalid shape. On the smart manipulator toolbar, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='validation_invalid.png'] button, and then click the Synchronize Manually command.
- In the Validation Results window, click the Solve button and then click the Synchronize Manually command. For more information on how to open the Validation Results window, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation' space='MD2024x'] .

###### The source and target elements area

The following table describes the buttons in the source and target areas of the Synchronization dialog.

| Button | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Source elements area: |  |
| Up | Click to shift the selected source element up a row. In this way you can change the order of the source elements. |
| Down | Click to shift the selected source element down a row. In this way you can change the order of the source elements. |
| Remove | Click to remove the selected source element from the project. |
| Edit | Click to open the Specification window of the selected source element. |
| Target elements area: |  |
| Up | Click to shift the selected target element up a row. In this way you can change the order of the target elements. |
| Down | Click to shift the selected target element down a row. In this way you can change the order of the target elements. |
| Remove | Click to remove the selected target element from the project. |
| Edit | Click to open the Specification window of the selected target element. |

###### Buttons in the Synchronization dialog

The following table describes the Synchronization dialog buttons.

| Button | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Synchronize All | Click the button to synchronize all the not synchronized elements. Then from the menu select the straightforward or backward synchronization. For example, when synchronizing Parameters (Activity) and Pins (Call Behavior Action) you can select to synchronize one of the following:Pins (Call Behavior Action) by Parameters (Activity)Parameters (Activity) by Pins (Call Behavior Action) |
| Synchronize Selected | Click to synchronize the selected parameter. Note that the button is disabled if no parameters are selected in the list.After you click the Synchronize Selected button, the following menu appears:Create -> - Click to create the target element with the same properties as the source element.<-Create - Click to create the source element to be created with the same properties as the target element.Update -> - Click to update the not synchronized properties of the target element according to the properties of the source element.<-Update - Click to update the not synchronized properties of the source element according to the properties of the target element.Note that the Update command exists only if there are properties to update. |

#### PANEL: On this page

On this page

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Parameters synchronization' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with model elements' space='MD2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The <strong>Synchronization</strong> dialog represents those elements whose parameters are not synchronized and provides the ability to restore the synchronization.</p><p>In this section, you will find brief information for opening the <strong>Synchronization</strong> dialog and for each button that can be useful while working in the dialog.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Synchronization dialog" ac:alt="Synchronization dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="parameter_synch.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Synchronization dialog environment" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p> </p><p>To open the Synchronization dialog, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>In the Model Browser, right-click the invalid element. On the shortcut menu, point to <strong>Validation</strong>, then to the validation group, and click the <strong>Synchronize Manually</strong> command.</li><li>On the diagram pane, select the invalid shape. On the smart manipulator toolbar, click the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="validation_invalid.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Synchronization dialog environment" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button, and then click the <strong>Synchronize Manually</strong> command.</li><li>In the <strong>Validation Results</strong> window, click the <strong>Solve</strong> button and then click the <strong>Synchronize Manually</strong> command. For more information on how to open the <strong>Validation Results</strong> window, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Validation" /></ac:link>.</li></ul><h4>The source and target elements area</h4><p>The following table describes the buttons in the source and target areas of the Synchronization dialog.</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Button</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td colspan="2"><strong>Source elements area:</strong></td></tr><tr><td>Up</td><td><p>Click to shift the selected source element up a row. In this way you can change the order of the source elements.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Down</td><td><p>Click to shift the selected source element down a row. In this way you can change the order of the source elements.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Remove</td><td><p>Click to remove the selected source element from the project.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Edit</td><td><p>Click to open the Specification window of the selected source element.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="2"><strong>Target elements area:</strong></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span>Up</span></td><td colspan="1"><p>Click to shift the selected target element up a row. In this way you can change the order of the target elements.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span>Down</span></td><td colspan="1"><p>Click to shift the selected target element down a row. In this way you can change the order of the target elements.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span>Remove</span></td><td colspan="1"><p>Click to remove the selected target element from the project.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span>Edit</span></td><td colspan="1"><p>Click to open the Specification window of the selected target element.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><h4>Buttons in the Synchronization dialog</h4><p>The following table describes the Synchronization dialog buttons.</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Button</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Synchronize All</strong></p></td><td><p>Click the button to synchronize all the not synchronized elements. Then from the menu select the straightforward or backward synchronization. For example, when synchronizing Parameters (Activity) and Pins (Call Behavior Action) you can select to synchronize one of the following:</p><ul><li>Pins (Call Behavior Action) by Parameters (Activity)</li><li>Parameters (Activity) by Pins (Call Behavior Action)</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Synchronize Selected</strong></p></td><td><p>Click to synchronize the selected parameter. Note that the button is disabled if no parameters are selected in the list.</p><p>After you click the Synchronize Selected button, the following menu appears:<br /><strong>Create -&gt;</strong> - <strong>Click</strong> to create the target element with the same properties as the source element.<br /><strong>&lt;-Create</strong> - Click to create the source element to be created with the same properties as the target element.<br /><strong>Update -&gt;</strong> - Click to update the not synchronized properties of the target element according to the properties of the source element.<br /><strong>&lt;-Update</strong> - Click to update the not synchronized properties of the source element according to the properties of the target element.</p><p>Note that the Update command exists only if there are properties to update.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a9a36f92-37a4-4db1-a58d-8aea935ced28"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">On this page</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p> <ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="640cb8fa-31dd-4323-99bc-fa42a94cd8a6" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="44ba6805-c2bb-45ad-83b9-cbc485ec676b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Parameters synchronization" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Working with model elements" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136714332 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01238: Syntax rules

- page_id: `136714332`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136714332/Syntax+rules
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:16:23.671+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Generating reports from the command line
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1333700293

1333700294

1333700293

- Command parameters containing spaces must be enclosed in quotes, for example, "UML Standard Profile".
- Command parameters are case sensitive, except project, imageFormat and recursive which are not case-sensitive.
- Blank spaces (“ “) separate commands and parameters.
- To specify a package name that contains a semicolon (;), you can use a backslash (\). For example, the package name “com;nomagic” can be typed by using “com\;nomagic”. The backslash followed by a semicolon (\;) is not considered to be a character separator.
- For a parameter that contains unicode characters such as Thai, you can use the xml properties file to generate a report. The properties file does not support the unicode encoding.
- A field entry consists of a name-value pair. The name and value formats are specified by [name=value] pattern strings. A valid name must start with a letter (a-z or A-Z) and can be followed by any letter, digit, or underscore.
- You can specify a field name that consists of brackets ( [ ] ) by using backslashes ( \ ). For example, "[author=NoMagic][Revision=[1.0]]" will be typed as "[author=NoMagic][Revision=\[1.0\]]".
- Either -package or -element can be used with the Generate command. For example, you can use -package without -element.
- If -upload is specified to the command line. Its value is validated and executed after a report has been generated.

1333700292

**Related pages**

- Generating a report from Teamwork Cloud Server
- Properties filename
- Uploading generated reports to servers
- Using the Generate command to generate reports

For example, the package name “com;nomagic” can be typed by using “com\;nomagic”. The backslash followed by a semicolon (\;) is not considered to be a character separator.</li><li>For a parameter that contains unicode characters such as Thai, you can use the xml properties file to generate a report. The properties file does not support the unicode encoding.</li><li>A field entry consists of a name-value pair. The name and value formats are specified by [name=value] pattern strings. A valid name must start with a letter (a-z or A-Z) and can be followed by any letter, digit, or underscore.</li><li>You can specify a field name that consists of brackets ( [ ] ) by using backslashes ( \ ). For example, &quot;[author=NoMagic][Revision=[1.0]]&quot; will be typed as &quot;[author=NoMagic][Revision=\[1.0\]]&quot;.</li><li>Either -package or -element can be used with the Generate command. For example, you can use -package without -element.</li><li>If -upload is specified to the command line. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710129 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01239: Table Criteria area

- page_id: `136710129`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710129/Table+Criteria+area
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:12:27.068+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Tables
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1261086216

1261086218

1261086217

The **Criteria** area appears below the table toolbars. If it is not displayed, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] in the table toolbar to expand it.

To select the specified value in the Model Browser or to open its Specification window, you can right-click on the value boxes in the **Criteria** area.

The following table describes the contents of the Criteria area according to different tables and provides references to procedures when trying to specify any box of this area.

| Table name where the box can be found | Box Name | Description | How to... |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Generic TableGlossary Table | Element Type | Click to open the Select Element Type dialog and select one or more types of elements you need to display in the Generic table. |  |
| Metric Table | Metric Suite | Click to open the Select Metric Suite dialog and select the metric suites you want to use for calculating the metrics of your model.If the Metric Table is created following the procedure described in , the Metric Suite box has one or more default values. Otherwise, you must specify the metric suite criteria to start working with the Metric Table. |  |
| Instance Table | Classifier | Click to open the Select Classifier dialog and select one or more classifiers whose instances you need to display in the Instance Table. |  |
| All tables | Scope (optional) | Click to select one or more packages, which contain the elements you need to display in the table.Click to open the Query dialog and specify the criteria that define your scope. |  |
| All tables | Filter | Type the phrase by which the element should be filtered and either press Enter or click the button.To open the menu with more filter options, click in the Filter box.To reset filter options, click in the Filter box. |  |

1261086215

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710106 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01240: Table toolbars

- page_id: `136710106`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710106/Table+toolbars
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:12:25.971+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Tables
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

1260269502

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

1260269505

1260269500

**On this page**

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

1260269504

#### NOTE: Important

Important

If the table toolbars are not available in a server project, lock the table for editing. Make sure you have the right to edit the model of this project.

##### Basic toolbar buttons

The following table describes the toolbar buttons found in tables.

| Button | Shortcut keys | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Navigation toolbar |  |  |
|  | Alt+B | Opens the in the and selects the current diagram or symbol in the Containment tree. |
| Edit toolbar |  |  |
| Add New > Create | Insert | Click to create a new element in the table. This element will also be added to the model. If several element types are available, a shortcut menu with available types will open. You will be able to select an element of the type you need.NoteIn Instance Table, expand and select to add a new instance. If this button is not active, the classifier is not specified for the instance table.In Metric Table, click Calculate Metrics and select Add New Metric with Different Parameters to create a new row with copied parameters. |
| Add Existing | Ctrl+Insert | Click to add an element from a model. The Select Element dialog opens. Only elements of the type defined in a specific table will be listed.NoteIn Instance Table, expand to find this button. |
| Delete | Ctrl+D | Click to remove selected elements both from the table and the model.NoteIn Instance Table, expand to find this button. |
| Remove From Table | Ctrl+Delete | Click to remove only selected elements from the table.NoteIn Instance Table, expand to find this button. |
| Layout toolbar |  |  |
|  |  | Click to expand or collapse rows when the Display Mode under the Options button is selected as Compact tree or Complete tree.You may expand the command and select:Expand Selected Recursively - to expand the selected row recursively.Expand All - to expand all rows.Collapse Selected Recursively - to collapse the selected row recursively.Collapse All - to collapse all rows. |
|  | Ctrl+Up Arrow | Click to shift selected elements (either grouped or non-grouped) up a row. The elements are automatically renumbered after moving. |
|  | Ctrl+Down Arrow | Click to shift selected elements (either grouped or non-grouped) down a row. The elements are automatically renumbered after moving. |
| Columns | N/A | Click to change the set of columns to show on the table. |
| Publish toolbar |  |  |
| Export | N/A | Click to export the contents of the table to an *.html, *.csv, or *.xlsx file.NoteIn the Metric Table this button also allows you export to *.xlsm file. |
| Excel/CSV Sync | N/A | Click to open the Excel/CSV Sync menu:Excel/CSV File - contains three commands: - Select File - opens the Excel/CSV Sync Options dialog and allows you to select a file from your file system or from your model to link it with the modeling tool table and enable data syncing. - Clear Selection - removes the reference between the modeling tool table and linked file and syncing is turned off. - Open File - opens the linked file.Read from File - imports data from Excel/CSV file to modeling tool table.Write to File - exports data from modeling tool table to Excel/CSV file.Sync Options - opens the Excel/CSV Sync Options dialog and allows you to customize sync and mapping options before syncing data between Excel/CSV file and modeling tool table. |
| Find in Diagram |  |  |
| Find in Diagram | Ctrl+F | Click to open the search bar allowing you to search for textual information in all types of diagrams, including tables, matrices, and maps. Use / to navigate the search results. |
| View toolbar |  |  |
|  | N/A | Click to update the contents of the table after specifying the scope.NoteIn the Instance Table, this button updates data after specifying the smart package as the scope criteria. |
| Validation toolbar |  |  |
|  | N/A | Click to validate the table against all validation rules stored in the model, except those that are ignored. |
| Options toolbar |  |  |
|  | N/A | Click to open the Options menu. Each table contains different options under this button. Below are the meanings of all available options:Show Full Paths – displays the full paths of elements (where owner information can be displayed).Save Filter Criteria – keeps the unchanged after reloading the project or after restarting MagicDraw.Show Detailed Column Name – in a generic table, shows property group and/or stereotype names in the table header. This is helpful when the table displays several columns with the same name. In a metric table, it shows the names of metric suites in column headers but it works when the metric table represents the metrics of two or more metric suites. In an instance table, it shows classifier names in column headers.Show Column Type – in a metric table, shows types of metric and parameter values in column headers. For example, Blocks Count : Integer. In an instance table, it shows types of slot values in column headers. For example, Year : Integer or Boat.Beam : String.Show Column Icons - shows icons in the column headers.Show Abbreviation in Column Type – in a metric table, shows abbreviations of metric or parameter names in column headers, if they are specified.Show Units on Values - in an instance table, shows unit symbols next to values.Show Units on Columns - in an instance table, it shows unit symbols in column headers.Wrap Text - wraps long text in table cells. The option is disabled by default. Limit Row Height - strips cell entries that cover more than 10 element values or plain text lines. Three dots are added at the end of the stripped cell. The complete text is available in the tooltip or in the cell edit mode.Load Partially - limits data calculation and loading to only visible cells in tables.Include Additional Content - includes additional content of elements that fall within the specified scope in tables. For example, if a Smart Package comes within the scope of the table, its content is collected and represented in the table.Enable Patterns-Based Verification - performs patterns-based Requirements verification.Display Mode - allows selection of one of the following data visualization options:- Complete tree - displays elements hierarchically with all their owners in the column header.- Compact tree - displays elements hierarchically with their direct and common owners in the column header.To learn more, visit the page.- List - displays elements in a plain list.Use Acronyms - recognizes acronyms everywhere in the text. Only capitalized terms are recognized as acronyms. Lowercase words are not interpreted as acronyms.Specification – opens the of an appropriate table. |
| Suppress/Expand Criteria Area toolbar |  |  |
|  | N/A | Click to hide the Criteria area. |
|  | N/A | Click to show the Criteria area. |
| Other buttons |  |  |
|  | Alt+Left Arrow | Click to navigate to the previously opened diagram, table, dependency matrix, or relation map. |
|  | Alt+Right Arrow | Click to navigate to the previously opened diagram, table, dependency matrix, or relation map. |

##### Specific toolbar buttons

The following table describes the toolbar buttons found only in specific tables.

| Table name where button can be found | Button | Shortcut keys | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | Edit toolbar |  |  |
| Instance Table | > Create with Parts | Alt+Insert | Click to create a new instance with parts. |
| Instance Table | > Delete with Parts | Alt+D | Click to remove selected elements both from the table and the model. |
| Metric Table | Calculate Metrics | N/A | Click to open the Calculate Metrics menu and then select the . |
| Requirement Table | Add Nested | Alt+Insert | Click to add a nested Requirement in the model. |
|  | Layout toolbar |  |  |
| Metric Table | Show Metric Suites | N/A | Click to select the metric suites whose columns you need to show on the Metric table. |
|  | Other buttons |  |  |
| Glossary Table |  | N/A | Click to turn the glossary on/off in the project. |

1260279301
