<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f207f992-a521-4085-a1c6-3b4e0d5e002d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1260269502</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="866432d8-cb4c-4cc0-8702-b50377585e8d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1260269505</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c837c3e1-ac5f-4835-9d0c-cb6a6cb95a57"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1260269500</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a89f80e7-fc4f-4df7-bf58-8149d8a378cb" /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aac3b9ca-c8c3-4211-a31d-984d1025d7a1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1260269504</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ed0f8018-afcd-4b2e-8a50-2c10922aae39"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the table toolbars are not available in a server project, lock the table for editing. Make sure you have the right to edit the model of this project.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Basic toolbar buttons</h3><p>The following table describes the toolbar buttons found in tables.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 84.9762%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 13.1898%;" /><col style="width: 10.4869%;" /><col style="width: 55.3511%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th style="text-align: left;">Button</th><th style="text-align: left;">Shortcut keys</th><th style="text-align: left;">Description</th></tr><tr><td class="highlight-blue" colspan="3" data-highlight-colour="blue"><strong>Navigation toolbar</strong></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:title="Select in Containment tree" ac:alt="Select in Containment tree"><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_in_containment_tree.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Alt+B</td><td><p><span>Opens the </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Containment tab" /></ac:link><span> in the</span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Model Browser" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ Model Browser]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span> and selects the current diagram or symbol in the Containment tree.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-blue" colspan="3" data-highlight-colour="blue"><strong>Edit toolbar</strong></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="23"><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_new_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Table toolbars" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="657e3bda-417a-4497-8533-a37fd9daa9f4">Add New</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="instance_table_add_new_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Table toolbars" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> &gt; <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="23"><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_new_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Table toolbars" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>Create</p></div></td><td>Insert</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Click to create a new element in the table. This element will also be added to the model. If several element types are available, a shortcut menu with available types will open. You will be able to select an element of the type you need.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a88375f8-df5f-455e-a02d-02ead856ef21"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>In Instance Table, expand <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="21"><ri:attachment ri:filename="instance_table_add_new_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Table toolbars" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="22"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Instance_table_create.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Table toolbars" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>to add a new instance.<span> If this <span>button</span> is not active, the classifier is not specified for the instance table.</span></li><li>In Metric Table, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="calc_metrics.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Table toolbars" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>Calculate Metrics and select <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_metric_with_diff_pars.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Table toolbars" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span> Add New Metric with Different Parameters to create a new row with copied parameters.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_existing_icon.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Table toolbars" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> Add Existing</p></div></td><td>Ctrl+Insert</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Click to add an element from a model. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Selecting elements" /><ac:link-body><strong>Select Element</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens. Only elements of the type defined in a specific table will be listed.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7aa8579b-bfee-419b-9b40-5708ce7f2202"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>In Instance Table, expand <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="21"><ri:attachment ri:filename="instance_table_add_new_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Table toolbars" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to find this button.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="gt_delete.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> Delete</p></div></td><td>Ctrl+D</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Click to remove selected elements both from the table and the model.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="384f50b4-dfc4-4247-aa16-6f64924e4a46"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>In Instance Table, expand <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="delete_in_instance_table.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Table toolbars" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to find this button.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="gt_delete_from_table.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>Remove From Table </p></div></td><td>Ctrl+Delete</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Click to remove only selected elements from the table.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3d45166e-66cf-464a-ac12-fc815f4c12dd"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>In Instance Table, expand <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="delete_in_instance_table.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Table toolbars" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to find this button.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-blue" colspan="3" data-highlight-colour="blue"><strong>Layout toolbar</strong></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:title="Expand Selected Recursively" ac:alt="Expand Selected Recursively"><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand_selected_recursively.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Table toolbars" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td colspan="1"><br /></td><td colspan="1"><p>Click to expand or collapse rows when the <strong>Display Mode</strong> under the <strong>Options</strong> button is selected as <strong>Compact tree</strong> or <strong>Complete tree</strong>.</p><p><span>You may expand the command and select:</span></p><ul><li><strong>Expand Selected Recursively</strong> - to expand the selected row recursively.</li><li><strong>Expand All</strong> - to expand all rows.</li><li><strong>Collapse Selected Recursively</strong> - to collapse the selected row recursively.</li><li><strong>Collapse All</strong> - to collapse all rows.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:title="Move the selected row up" ac:alt="Move the selected row up"><ri:attachment ri:filename="gt_up.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Ctrl+Up Arrow</td><td><p>Click to shift selected elements (either grouped or non-grouped) up a row. The elements are automatically renumbered after moving.</p></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:title="Move the selected row down" ac:alt="Move the selected row down"><ri:attachment ri:filename="gt_down.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td><p>Ctrl+Down Arrow</p></td><td><p>Click to shift selected elements (either grouped or non-grouped) down a row. The elements are automatically renumbered after moving.</p></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="gt_show_columns.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> Columns</p></div></td><td><span>N/A</span></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Click to change the set of columns to show on the table.</p></div></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-blue" colspan="3" data-highlight-colour="blue"><strong>Publish toolbar</strong></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="gt_export.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> Export</p></div></td><td colspan="1">N/A</td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Click to export the contents of the table to an <em>*.html</em>, <em>*.csv</em>, or <em>*.xlsx</em> file.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="56ae61b6-63c3-4254-b3e3-3104fa8b8050"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>In the Metric Table this button also allows you export to *.xlsm file.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="excel_csv_sync.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>Excel/CSV Sync</p></div></td><td colspan="1">N/A</td><td colspan="1"><p>Click to open the Excel/CSV Sync menu:</p><ul><li><strong>Excel/CSV File</strong> - contains three commands:<br /> - <strong>Select File</strong> - opens the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Excel and CSV Sync Options dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Excel/CSV Sync Options</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> and allows you to select a file from your file system or from your model to link it with the modeling tool table and enable data syncing.<br /> - <strong>Clear Selection</strong> - removes the reference between the modeling tool table and linked file and syncing is turned off.<br /> - <strong>Open File</strong> - opens the linked file.</li><li><strong>Read from File</strong> - imports data from Excel/CSV file to modeling tool table.</li><li><strong>Write to File</strong> - exports data from modeling tool table to Excel/CSV file.</li><li><strong>Sync Options</strong> - opens the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Excel and CSV Sync Options dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Excel/CSV Sync Options</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> and allows you to customize sync and mapping options before syncing data between Excel/CSV file and modeling tool table.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-blue" colspan="1" data-highlight-colour="blue"><strong>Find in Diagram</strong></td><td class="highlight-blue" colspan="1" data-highlight-colour="blue"><br /></td><td class="highlight-blue" colspan="1" data-highlight-colour="blue"><br /></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="13"><ri:attachment ri:filename="find_in_diagram_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Searching in diagrams" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> Find in Diagram</p></div></td><td colspan="1">Ctrl+F</td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Click to open the search bar allowing you to search for textual information in <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">all types of diagrams, including tables, matrices, and maps. Use <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="10"><ri:attachment ri:filename="find_previous.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Searching in diagrams" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>/<ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="9"><ri:attachment ri:filename="find_next.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Searching in diagrams" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to navigate the search results.</span></p></div></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-blue" colspan="3" data-highlight-colour="blue"><strong>View toolbar</strong></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:title="Refresh" ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td colspan="1">N/A</td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Click to update the contents of the table after specifying the scope.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="015220a7-5ae8-4918-acd1-de273a0596e4"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>In the Instance Table, this button updates data after specifying the smart package as the scope criteria.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-blue" colspan="3" data-highlight-colour="blue"><strong>Validation toolbar</strong></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:title="Validate Diagram" ac:alt="Validate Diagram"><ri:attachment ri:filename="validate_diagram.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </p></div></td><td colspan="1">N/A</td><td colspan="1">Click to validate the table against all validation rules stored in the model, except those that are ignored.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-blue" colspan="3" data-highlight-colour="blue"><strong>Options toolbar</strong></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:title="Options" ac:alt="Options"><ri:attachment ri:filename="options_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </p></div></td><td colspan="1">N/A</td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Click to open the Options menu. Each table contains different options under this button. Below are the meanings of all available options:</p><ul><li><strong>Show Full Paths</strong> – displays the full paths of elements (where owner information can be displayed).</li><li><strong>Save Filter Criteria</strong> – keeps the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Quick filter" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[filter criteria]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> unchanged after reloading the project or after restarting MagicDraw.</li><li><strong>Show Detailed Column Name</strong> – in a generic table, shows property group and/or stereotype names in the table header. This is helpful when the table displays several columns with the same name. In a metric table, it shows the names of metric suites in column headers but it works when the metric table represents the metrics of two or more metric suites. In an instance table, it shows classifier names in column headers.</li><li><strong>Show Column Type</strong> – in a metric table, shows types of metric and parameter values in column headers. For example, <em>Blocks Count : Integer</em>. In an instance table, it shows types of slot values in column headers. For example, <em>Year : Integer</em> or <em>Boat.Beam : String</em>.</li><li><strong>Show Column Icons</strong> - shows icons in the column headers.</li><li><strong>Show Abbreviation in Column Type</strong> – in a metric table, shows abbreviations of metric or parameter names in column headers, if they are specified.</li><li><span><strong>Show Units on Values</strong> -<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> in an instance table, shows unit symbols next to values</span>.</span></li><li><span><strong>Show Units on Columns</strong> - <span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">in an instance table, it</span></span> shows unit symbols in column headers.</span></li><li><span><strong>Wrap Text</strong> - wraps long text in<strong> </strong>table cells. The option is disabled by default. </span></li><li><span><span><strong>Limit Row Height</strong> - strips </span></span>cell entries that cover more than 10 element values or plain text lines. Three dots are added at the end of the stripped cell. The complete text is available in the tooltip or in the cell edit mode.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Load Partially</strong> - limits data calculation and loading to only visible cells in tables.</p></li><li><strong>Include Additional Content</strong> - includes additional content of elements that fall within the specified scope in tables. For example, if a Smart Package comes within the scope of the table, its content is collected and represented in the table.</li><li><strong>Enable Patterns-Based Verification</strong> - performs patterns-based Requirements verification.</li><li><span><span><strong>Display Mode</strong> - allows selection of one of the following data visualization options:<br /></span></span></li></ul><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">- <strong>Complete tree</strong> - displays elements hierarchically with all their owners in the column header.<strong><br /></strong>- <strong>Compact tree</strong> - displays elements hierarchically with their direct and common owners in the column header.<br />To learn more, visit the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Hierarchy in tables" /></ac:link> page.<strong><br /></strong>- <strong>List</strong> - displays elements in a plain list.</p><ul><li><span><strong>Use Acronyms</strong> - recognizes acronyms everywhere in the text. Only capitalized terms are recognized as acronyms. Lowercase words are not interpreted as acronyms.</span><span><br /></span></li><li><strong>Specification </strong><span>– opens the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link> of an appropriate table.</span><span><br /></span></li></ul></div></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-blue" colspan="3" data-highlight-colour="blue"><strong>Suppress/Expand Criteria Area toolbar</strong></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:title="Suppress/Expand Criteria Area"><ri:attachment ri:filename="gt_supress.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td colspan="1"><span>N/A</span></td><td colspan="1">Click to hide the <strong>Criteria</strong> area.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:title="Suppress/Expand Criteria Area"><ri:attachment ri:filename="gt_expand.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td colspan="1"><span>N/A</span></td><td colspan="1">Click to show the <strong>Criteria</strong> area.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-blue" colspan="3" data-highlight-colour="blue"><strong>Other buttons</strong></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:title="Back" ac:alt="Back"><ri:attachment ri:filename="back_diagram_toolbar.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </p></div></td><td colspan="1">Alt+Left Arrow</td><td colspan="1"><p>Click to navigate to the previously opened diagram, table, dependency matrix, or relation map.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:title="Forward" ac:alt="Forward"><ri:attachment ri:filename="forward_diagram_toolbar.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </p></div></td><td colspan="1"><p>Alt+Right Arrow</p></td><td colspan="1"><p>Click to navigate to the previously opened diagram, table, dependency matrix, or relation map.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><h3>Specific toolbar buttons</h3><p>The following table describes the toolbar buttons found only in specific tables.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 89.4277%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 17.7714%;" /><col style="width: 26.0439%;" /><col style="width: 12.7106%;" /><col style="width: 43.4356%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th colspan="1">Table name where button can be found</th><th>Button</th><th>Shortcut keys</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td class="highlight-blue" colspan="3" data-highlight-colour="blue"><strong>Edit toolbar</strong></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Instance Table</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_in_instance_table.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Table toolbars" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> &gt; <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_new.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Table toolbars" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> Create with Parts</p></div></td><td><span>Alt+Insert</span></td><td>Click <span>to create a new instance with parts.</span></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Instance Table</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="delete_in_instance_table.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Table toolbars" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> &gt; <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="gt_delete.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Table toolbars" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>Delete with Parts</p></div></td><td><span>Alt+D</span></td><td>Click <span><span><span>to remove selected elements both from the table and the model.</span></span></span></td></tr><tr><td>Metric Table</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="calc_metrics.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Table toolbars" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>Calculate Metrics</p></div></td><td>N/A</td><td>Click to open the Calculate Metrics menu and then select the <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Metric table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[needed command]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span>.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Requirement Table</td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="22"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2019-7-19_10-49-55.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Table toolbars" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> Add Nested</p></div></td><td colspan="1">Alt+Insert</td><td colspan="1">Click to add a nested Requirement in the model.</td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td class="highlight-blue" colspan="3" data-highlight-colour="blue"><strong>Layout toolbar</strong></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Metric Table</td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_metric_suites.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Table toolbars" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> Show Metric Suites</p></div></td><td colspan="1">N/A</td><td colspan="1">Click to select the metric suites whose columns you need to show on the Metric table.</td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td class="highlight-blue" colspan="3" data-highlight-colour="blue"><strong>Other buttons</strong></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Glossary Table</td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:title="Turn on/off to use the glossary in the project"><ri:attachment ri:filename="glossary.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Table toolbars" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </p></div></td><td colspan="1">N/A</td><td colspan="1">Click to turn the glossary on/off in the project.</td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="993ae71d-a86a-4404-9fd2-b9d9670f8dab"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1260279301</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><strong><br <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710632 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01241: Table with diagram information

- page_id: `136710632`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710632/Table+with+diagram+information
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:12:48.114+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

569464989

569464992

569464991

On the diagram, you can display a table containing various diagram details: its name, author, status, the dates it was created and modified, etc. By default, the Diagram info table is displayed at the right top corner of the diagram frame, but you can drag and drop it to any other position on the diagram.

The table includes the following fields:

- Diagram name
- Author
- Creation date
- Modification date
- Last Modified by
- Other available tag definitions

To show the table containing the diagram information

- From the diagram shortcut menu, select Show Diagram Info .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To define information for the table

1. Do one of the following:
  - From the diagram information table shortcut menu, select Customize.
  - From the Options menu, select Project .
2. In the Project Options dialog > Diagram Info > Source , select the type of information you want to include in the table:
  - Standard Mode . This mode contains the following fields that will be shown in the table: Author, Creation date, Modification date, and all other tag definitions that can be assigned to the diagram.
  - Custom Mode . In this mode, you can create your own table or any other object in HTML.
3. Preview the selected table or other created object in the Preview .

569464988

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagramming' space='MD2024x']

the table containing the diagram information</p><hr /><ul><li>From the diagram shortcut menu, select <strong>Show Diagram Info</strong>.</li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="digraminfo.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Table with diagram information" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p> </p><p>To define information for the table</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>From the diagram information table shortcut menu, select <strong>Customize.</strong></li><li>From the <strong>Options</strong> menu, select <strong>Project</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>In the Project Options dialog &gt; <strong>Diagram Info</strong> &gt; <strong>Source</strong>, select the type of information you want to include in the table: <ul><li><strong>Standard Mode</strong>. This mode contains the following fields that will be shown in the table: Author, Creation date, Modification date, and all other tag definitions that can be assigned to the diagram. </li><li><strong>Custom Mode</strong>. In this mode, you can create your own table or any other object in HTML.</li></ul></li><li>Preview the selected table or other created object in the <strong>Preview</strong>.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6ff23793-ea88-4a9f-a379-0783a6827205"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">569464988</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Diagramming" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 01242: Tableprop tool

- page_id: `136714052`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136714052/Tableprop+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:16:04.336+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Helper tools
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

1586165640

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

1586165642

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

1586165641

**$tableprop** is a table property tool to manage table style. In this version, you can set merged rows vertically and columns horizontally in *DOCX*,*PPTX*, and*XLSX* templates using **$tableprop**.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

This statement must be defined in a table.

1586165638

**Related pages**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f94fe3a4-4f5d-4bf5-8c07-ca904fca6d8f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1586165640</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d3826422-1139-4ef2-b3db-c748e444aca2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1586165642</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ecd21a5a-8560-4a3d-a68a-00cf03e27480"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1586165641</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>$tableprop</strong> is a table property tool to manage table style. In this version, you can set merged rows vertically and columns horizontally in <em style="line-height: 1.42857;">DOCX</em>,<em style="line-height: 1.42857;"> PPTX</em>, and<em style="line-height: 1.42857;"> XLSX</em> templates using <strong>$tableprop</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="95a02093-3a51-4cf2-9982-031e8a405d48"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>This statement must be defined in a table.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2761a5c5-e3dc-4a2f-8a6f-a71177479064"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1586165638</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="599b290e-aba8-4f32-9142-1807ef9dcbed" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710091 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 01243: Tables

- page_id: `136710091`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710091/Tables
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-02-01T11:28:13.743+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1259844527

1259844530

1259844526

**On this page**

43

1259844528

##### Introduction

You can use modeling tool tables to represent and modify information about the properties of model elements and/or relationships between them. You can create four kinds of tables: Generic Table, Glossary Table, Metric Table, and Instance Table. The different purposes for each table are illustrated below:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generic table' space=''] allows you to manage selected elements and their properties in a single place. The example below is from a [ATTACHMENT filename='generic table.mdzip'] sample model. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Glossary table' space='']Glossary Table allows you to define the specific term(s) and gives you the ability to reuse the term(s) anywhere in your model. The example below is created only for this image and is not saved in any of the sample models. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instance table' space=''] allows you to manage an Instance Specification of your model in spreadsheet form. The example is from [ATTACHMENT filename='SpacecraftMassRollup.mdzip'] sample model. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Metric table' space='']Metric Table allows you to collect various metrics used to evaluate the current state of your model. The example below is from [ATTACHMENT filename='MagicLibrary requirements.mdzip'] sample model. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Table areas

Before working with tables, you should understand their structure. All tables contain the following areas:

- *Table toolbars* allow you to quickly access the options for managing data in the table. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Table toolbars' space='']>]]>
- *Criteria area* allows you to define the elements that you want to display in the table, specify the scope of the table, and filter necessary information in tables that contain large amounts of data. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Table Criteria area' space='']>]]>
- Grid displays data according to the defined [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Table Criteria area' space=''] . You can modify the displayed data in the grid by editing cells, removing, hiding, creating new, or selecting other options from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Table toolbars' space=''] . The grid consists of: - Rows that appear along the side of the table, and are labeled using numbers starting with 1. Each table row represents an element . 
- Columns that appear along the top of the table, and are labeled using element property names. Each table column represents element properties. - Cells are the intersections of every row and column, which represent element property values.
- Description area allows you to view the general information regarding the purpose and functions of the table.
- Table info area allows you to view the table display information. On the left, you can see the number of displayed and total rows; on the right, the status, indicating whether the table is still loading [ATTACHMENT filename='status_loading_2.png'] or fully loaded [ATTACHMENT filename='status_loading_complete_2.png'] .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1259860030

**Basic features of tables**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Table toolbars' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Table Criteria area' space='MD2024x']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711370 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01244: Tag

- page_id: `136711370`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711370/Tag
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:35.552+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Just like a class, a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MD2024x']can have properties, which can be referred to as tag definitions. When a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MD2024x']is applied to a model element, the values of the properties may be referred to as tagged values.

The tag definitions are used to define new meta attributes of the extended metaclass, they are used as regular class attributes.

An actual instance of the tag definition is a tagged value (tag).

A tag holds extra information like:

- additional information that does not come with UML, for example Precondition for Use Cases.
- management data about the state and progress of the project such as author, status, and tested.
- language specific data for code generation tools.

A tagged value consists of two parts: name and value (example: Author = Joe).

To create a new tag definition

1. Create a new stereotype.
2. Open the stereotype [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'] .
3. In the Tag Definitions property group, click the Create button to add a new tag definition for stereotype.
4. Select the type of this property. Type of the property can be a standard UML data type or another user defined Stereotype. Regular classes should not be used as types of tag definition.

#### PANEL: Related References

Related References

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='MD2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136712848 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01245: Tags multiplicity 2

- page_id: `136712848`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136712848/Tags+multiplicity+2
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:15:01.249+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Validation > UML validation rules > UML completeness constraints rules
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Abbreviation**

TagMult2

**Description**

This validation rule checks if the number of tag values is in the multiplicity range. The validation rule is triggered if there are fewer values than the set multiplicity range.

**Severity**

warning

**Constrained Element**

TaggedValue

**Solvers**

- To fix this, make sure the number of tag values is not lower than the specified multiplicity range.

**Example**

###### **[IMAGE alt='' src='']**The Stereotype *Car* has a tag definition *airbags* with a multiplicity of two or more. The class *Car Model 1*with the applied stereotype *Car*has the *airbags*tag specified with only one value, which causes an error. 
The error is resolved by specifying two values.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Abbreviation</strong></p><p>TagMult2</p><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>This validation rule checks if the number of tag values is in the multiplicity range. The validation rule is triggered if there are fewer values than the set multiplicity range.</p><p><strong>Severity</strong></p><p>warning</p><p><strong>Constrained Element</strong></p><p>TaggedValue</p><p><strong>Solvers</strong></p><ul><li>To fix this, make sure the number of tag values is not lower than the specified multiplicity range.</li></ul><p><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="844f9cdb-b9ab-4de6-af39-8d12da834add">Example</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Tags multiplicity 2.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Tags multiplicity 2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></strong>The Stereotype <em>Car</em> has a tag definition <em>airbags</em> with a multiplicity of two or more. The class <em>Car Model 1 </em>with the applied stereotype <em>Car </em>has the <em>airbags </em>tag specified with only one value, which causes an error.<br />The error is resolved by specifying two values.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136713424 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 01246: Teamwork Cloud data markings

- page_id: `136713424`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136713424/Teamwork+Cloud+data+markings
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2023-09-22T13:17:19.737+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Teamwork Cloud data markings help to classify resources with custom-defined access levels. They are shown if they were [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing data markings in Teamwork Cloud Admin' space='TWCloud2024x'].

You may see Teamwork Cloud data markings when you:

- try to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opening projects from Teamwork Cloud' space='MD2024x'] ,
- want to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing server projects' space='MD2024x'] , or
- need to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing project usages' space='MD2024x'] in your project.

If set, Teamwork Cloud data markings are displayed in an appropriate column of a dialog:

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Open Server Project dialog with Teamwork Cloud data markings.

If there is an access level set on a project and you want to use that project in your project, your project must have the same or more strict access level rights. Otherwise, you see a warning:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If you decide to use a used project with a stricter access level in your project, users must also have the same or stricter access level rights to be able to open your project.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Teamwork Cloud data markings help to classify resources with custom-defined access levels. They are shown if they were <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloud2024x" ri:content-title="Managing data markings in Teamwork Cloud Admin" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[enabled and created in Teamwork Cloud Admin]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p>You may see Teamwork Cloud data markings when you:</p><ul><li>try to <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Opening projects from Teamwork Cloud" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[open a server project from Teamwork Cloud]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>,</li><li>want to <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Managing server projects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[manage a server project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, or</li><li>need to <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Managing project usages" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ use a project ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>in your project.</li></ul><p>If set, Teamwork Cloud data markings are displayed in an appropriate column of a dialog:</p><h6><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="twc_data_markings.png" /></ac:image></h6><h6 style="text-align: center;">Open Server Project dialog with Teamwork Cloud data markings.</h6><p>If there is an access level set on a project and you want to use that project in your project, your project must have the same or more strict access level rights. Otherwise, you see a warning:</p><p><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="156" ac:width="639"><ri:attachment ri:filename="data_markings_warning.png" /></ac:image></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">If you decide to use a used project with a stricter access level in your project, users must also have the same or stricter access level rights to be able to open your project.</span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711372 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01247: Template

- page_id: `136711372`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711372/Template
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:35.823+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Working With Templates

502366679

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Working With Templates

502366682

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Working With Templates

502366680

Use a Template to define concrete types for elements without concrete types. You can create a template and then use it as many times as you need. Moreover, when you have elements of the same structure (or with the same parameters) but with different values, the template you use can easily define parameters for each element. You can apply template parametrization to classifiers, packages, and operations. Most often. template parametrization is used for classes and interfaces. Templates are often used in code engineering (Java and other programming languages).

The template consists of:

- A parameterized element (an element with defined template parameters). The parameterized element can also be called a template element.
- An actual element (an element for which a template is applied, an element with actual values). The actual element can also be called a templateable element.
- A template binding relationship (a relationship from the actual element to the parameterized element).
- A template parameter substitution (used to define actual parameters of the actual element that substitutes formal template parameters). Template parameter substitutions are created on the template binding relationship.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of Template

In this example, you can see the *GeneralArray*template class, which has the *ArraySize* and *ArrayType* template parameters, represented by the dashed rectangle in the upper-right corner of the class. These parameters represent the size and type of the general array.The attribute *GeneralArray::contents : ArrayType [0..ArraySize]* represents the *ArrayType* and the *ArraySize* template parameters. The *ArraySize* template parameter is of the Integer type, and its default value is 10. The *ArrayType* template parameter is of the Class type (it is not represented in the shape), and its default value is not specified. The default value of the template parameter is used if no actual value is supplied for the parameter in a binding. Typically, the parameter types are classifiers, but they can also be integers or other types.

The *GeneralArray* class and its template parameters create a template of the general array of undefined types. You can use this template in other concrete arrays as many times as you need.

How to use the template and how to create an actual element

To create an array of addresses, you would create the *AddressArray* class. Alone (without a template), the *AddressArray* class would have undefined types. A template binding is created from the *AddressArray* class to the *GeneralArray* class. Now the template binding connection points to the *GeneralArray* template class.

The template binding specifies template parameter substitutions, the actual values of parameters for the address array. In the example, we created the following actual values of the AdressArray class: the array size having the value 3 and the array of the *Address* type.

The output of these definitions results in the *AddressArray* class having the attribute *AddressArray::contents : Address [0..3]*.

Attributes for the actual element classes should be created manually. In the example, the*AddressArray::contents : Address [0..3]*attribute is not created automatically - you must create this attribute manually. Use these optional solutions to create these attributes in your model or not.

#### TIP: Working With Templates

Working With Templates

Step by step instructions for modelling a template in the MagicDraw are described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Template' space='MD2024x'].

You can format template parameter symbol properties in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MD2024x']**Symbol Properties** dialog.

You can specify template parameter properties in the Template Parameter [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'] and template binding properties in the Template Binding Specification window. In the specification window, you can find the description of each property. Descriptions are presented in the description area of the Specification window.

502366678

**Related pages**

- Model elements
- Auxiliary Diagram Symbols
- Diagramming
